chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

parts.text: سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُقَلِّمَ أَظَافِيرَهُ عِنْدَ اَلْإِحْرَامِ حَتَّى أَحْرَمَ

text: «يَدَعُهَا» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا أَفْتَاهُ أَنْ يُقَلِّمَ أَظَافِيرَهُ وَ يُعِيدَ إِحْرَامَهُ فَفَعَلَ فَقَالَ «عَلَيْهِ دَمٌ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2692 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about a man who forgot to trim his nails before entering the state of ihram until he had already entered it.
Imam (as) said: "He should leave them as they are."
I said: "One of our companions advised him to trim his nails and renew his ihram, so he did."
Imam (as) said: "He must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."
